title: Treehouse Of Horror Ix

jokes.text: D'oh! [Homer's signature exclamation after what appears to be a car accident]
jokes.setup: Car accident or mishap based on tire screeching and groaning
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase perfectly integrated into character moment, though overused by this point in series

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Let's see how she handles. [Chuckles] Oh, yeah. Tame it, baby.
jokes.setup: Snake examining something (likely a weapon) with technical assessment
jokes.punchline: Suddenly shifts to sexual innuendo about 'taming' it
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Snake's sleazy personality through weapon-as-sexual-metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, this baby's got a hair trigger. Real sensitive. You gotta know how to handle it.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Takes a real man to tame a beast like this, man.

jokes.text: Hey, 'Pu, you got a breakfast cereal for people with syphilis?
jokes.setup: Snake asking Apu about breakfast cereals
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and random disease-specific cereal request
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random and dark humor typical of Snake's character - unexpected disease reference makes it memorable

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Hey Apu, you got any of those breakfast cereals?

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: We have many cereals, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, you got that one for kids with severe anal bleeding?

jokes.text: First, you torched that orphanage, then you blew up that bus full of nuns. Hey, that was self-defense.
jokes.setup: Listing Snake's previous violent crimes involving innocent victims
jokes.punchline: Snake claiming destroying a bus full of nuns was 'self-defense'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with perfect character-appropriate punchline - Snake's casual dismissal is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You destroyed a bus full of nuns?

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Self-defense, man.

jokes.text: Well, you'll be seeing lots of nuns where you're going, pal- Hell! Because the penalty for strike three is death.
jokes.setup: Wiggum starting what sounds like a heaven reference about seeing nuns
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by saying 'Hell' then explaining it's about death penalty
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke playing with religious expectations and Wiggum's bumbling delivery style

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You know, I've seen things that would make a nun weep. Things so horrible, so depraved, that I wouldn't wish them on my worst enemy. I'm talking about Hell.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: The electric chair executions we used to have here in Springfield.

jokes.text: You kids crack me up.
jokes.setup: Just put Bart in mortal danger by identifying him as witness to Snake
jokes.punchline: Wiggum treats this as cute kid behavior rather than serious police incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum obliviousness - completely missing the gravity of endangering a child witness

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, well, well. Witness protection for a kid. That's cute. We'll just put him in a little disguise, maybe give him a fake name like... Jimbo.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Chief, isn't that dangerous?

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Dangerous? Nah. Kids do this kind of thing all the time. Why, just last week my boy Ralph pretended to be a mailman for three days. Nobody got hurt... much.

jokes.text: from the producers of When Skirts Fall Off and Secrets of National Security Revealed
jokes.setup: Introducing TV show credits
jokes.punchline: Fake previous shows with absurdly exploitative/inappropriate titles
jokes.explanation: Good satire of exploitative TV programming with cleverly escalating inappropriateness

dialogue.speaker: Ed McMahon
dialogue.line: And now, back to The Simpsons!

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: The following program contains scenes of mild peril.

jokes.text: How come they only do crucifixions during sweeps?
jokes.setup: Snake about to be executed, complaining about the electric chair
jokes.punchline: Casual complaint about TV scheduling of extreme executions like it's entertainment programming
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor combining TV industry knowledge with execution methods - Snake treating death like TV programming is perfect

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Man, they got me scheduled right in the middle of sweeps week. Do you know how many people are gonna be watching this? This is like, primetime execution, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: I mean, why couldn't they execute me on a Tuesday at 2 AM like a normal criminal? Now everybody's gonna see my final moments on live TV.

jokes.text: Dibs on the liver!
jokes.setup: Taking Snake's body for organ harvesting
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic 'dibs' call on specific organ like claiming playground equipment
jokes.explanation: Solid dark humor treating organ harvesting like casual competition - 'dibs' makes it childishly inappropriate

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, we can harvest his organs for transplant.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Dibs on the liver!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Dr. Nick, he's still alive.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Dibs!

jokes.text: This is genuine human hair. This is legal, right? Yeah, sure. Whatever.
jokes.setup: Medical procedure with questionable hair transplant
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick's dismissive 'yeah, sure, whatever' response to legality concerns
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick incompetence and casual disregard for medical ethics - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure, whatever.

jokes.text: If your fly weren't open, you'd look just like Roger Moore.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Homer's new suave appearance
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment noting his pants are undone
jokes.explanation: Well-structured joke with good setup/punchline timing - Lisa's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, you look really suave today. But... your pants are undone.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: I know you are, but what am I?
jokes.setup: Apu saying 'But you're dead' to Snake's spirit
jokes.punchline: Playground comeback completely inappropriate for supernatural revenge scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of childish comeback with supernatural horror - Snake's personality bleeding through Homer

dialogue.speaker: Apu
dialogue.line: But you're dead!

dialogue.speaker: Snake
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, your mom!

jokes.text: Looks to me like he suffered a lot, Chief. Ah, geez, Lou. How long were you gonna let me keep drinking this thing?
jokes.setup: Wiggum saying Apu didn't suffer at crime scene
jokes.punchline: Lou corrects him while Wiggum realizes he's been drinking something inappropriate at the crime scene
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum obliviousness combined with visual gag of inappropriate crime scene behavior

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Apu didn't suffer at all. He was killed instantly.

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Chief, I hate to break it to ya, but Apu was shot in the back. He definitely suffered.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, that explains why this donut tastes like formaldehyde.

jokes.text: Shut your squeal-hole, booze jockey. I'm gonna, like, totally waste you.
jokes.setup: Moe greeting Homer normally in the morning
jokes.punchline: Snake's voice and slang coming out of Homer, calling Moe 'booze jockey'
jokes.explanation: Good character fusion comedy - Snake's surfer slang with 'booze jockey' is creative insult for bartender

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Morning, Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey there, booze jockey!

jokes.text: Filthy old bartender Moe Szyslak has watered down his last highball.
jokes.setup: Announcing Moe's death on news
jokes.punchline: Death announcement as pun about his bartending practices
jokes.explanation: Solid news-speak pun connecting Moe's death to his professional failings as bartender

dialogue.speaker: Kent Brockman
dialogue.line: We interrupt this program to bring you breaking news. Moe Szyslak, Springfield's most beloved bartender, has passed away at the age of 61. Moe died doing what he loved best: mixing a drink behind the bar. He will be deeply missed by all who knew him, but not nearly as much as he will be missed by the bottle of whiskey he was holding when he went.

jokes.text: Stop it. You're killing me!
jokes.setup: Homer/Snake physically attacking Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart using common expression 'you're killing me' while literally being killed
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective literal/figurative wordplay - common expression becomes darkly literal

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: You're killing me!

jokes.text: Oh, please, Lisa. Everyone's already figured that out.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining the obvious plot about Snake's hair controlling Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge dismissively noting the audience has already figured out the simple plot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke breaking fourth wall - Simpsons acknowledging their own obvious plotting to audience

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, duh.

jokes.text: More than a lush head of hair? Don't make me choose!
jokes.setup: Snake asking if Homer loves his son more than hair
jokes.punchline: Homer's anguished response treating it as impossible choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer vanity moment - genuinely torn between son and hair in character-appropriate way

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why would you make me choose?! That's like asking me to choose between my son and my hair! I love them both equally... No wait, hair. Hair.

jokes.text: Apu Nahasa- pasa- Ah,just Moe.Just Moe.
jokes.setup: Wiggum trying to pronounce Apu's last name during arrest
jokes.punchline: Gives up on pronunciation, settles for just charging with Moe's murder
jokes.explanation: Solid Wiggum incompetence joke - can't handle foreign names so just simplifies the charges

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You're under arrest for the murder of Moe... uh... A-poo... Nah-poo-poo-poo... okay, forget it. You're under arrest for murder.

jokes.text: Now that's what I call a bad hair day.
jokes.setup: Snake's possessed hair just got destroyed by police
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's terrible pun about the supernatural hair incident
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pun that works because it's so predictably awful - Wiggum's dad-joke delivery sells it

jokes.text: Oh. Wait. I just got it. Bad hair day.
jokes.setup: Wiggum made terrible 'bad hair day' pun
jokes.punchline: Lou's delayed realization and appreciation of the awful pun
jokes.explanation: Decent callback extending the bad pun joke - Lou's slower uptake adds to the police incompetence theme

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Hey, wait a minute... that's funny!

jokes.text: Sorry, but if I let you watch one of these gruesome Halloween cartoons... I'd be a pretty lousy mother.
jokes.setup: Kids wanting to watch violent Halloween cartoon
jokes.punchline: Marge's concern about being lousy mother while family is constantly in deadly situations
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Marge's selective parenting concerns given their lifestyle of constant danger

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I'm a lousy mother. Here I am worried about the kids watching a little violence on TV, when our family is constantly in deadly, life-threatening situations.

jokes.text: Going where?
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning Halloween and Homer not going as hobo again
jokes.punchline: Homer's clueless response while presumably already in hobo costume
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - not understanding he's already dressed for Halloween trick-or-treating

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you're not going as a hobo again this year, are you?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A hobo? Why would I go as a hobo? I'm going as a bum!

jokes.text: Don't you ever get tired of being wrong?
jokes.setup: Lisa warning Bart about plutonium being dangerous
jokes.punchline: Bart's confident dismissal right before presumably getting zapped
jokes.explanation: Good setup for immediate comeuppance - Bart's arrogance perfectly timed before consequences hit

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, that plutonium is extremely dangerous. One exposure could be fatal.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba! I'm not worried. What's the worst that could happen?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Eat my shorts, radiation!

jokes.text: Hey, Lise, we're characters in a cartoon. How humiliating.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa realizing they're now animated cartoon characters
jokes.punchline: Lisa's deadpan reaction treating being in cartoon as humiliating
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor with Lisa's character-appropriate reaction to their cartoon status as social embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait a minute, Lisa. We're animated.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I'm aware, Bart. And I'm deeply ashamed.

jokes.text: Hey. They're laughing at your pain. That's mean. Let's teach 'em a lesson.
jokes.setup: Observing audience laughing at their cartoon violence
jokes.punchline: Deciding to attack the audience for enjoying their suffering
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-commentary on cartoon violence and audience complicity - characters breaking fourth wall to attack viewers

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, they're laughing at us getting hurt!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Yeah, they think our pain is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Well, if they enjoy watching us suffer, let's give 'em something to really laugh about!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: You mean...?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That's right. Let's attack the audience!

jokes.text: To protect and sever
jokes.setup: Police car motto should read 'To Protect and Serve'
jokes.punchline: Changed to 'To Protect and Sever' in cartoon violence context
jokes.explanation: Good visual pun playing on police motto in violent cartoon context - works on rewatch when spotted

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Our new police car motto should read 'To Protect and Serve'.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: But it says 'To Protect and Sever'.

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Wiggedy, wiggedy. Poochie's in the house.
jokes.setup: Random appearance of Poochie character
jokes.punchline: His outdated 'radical' catchphrase in inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Great callback to previous episode's corporate-created 'cool' character - perfectly inappropriate timing

dialogue.speaker: Poochie
dialogue.line: Yo, yo, yo! Poochie here! That's radical!

jokes.text: I'm telling you, this cilantro really gives it a zing! Reg, there's no cilantro in it.
jokes.setup: TV host praising cilantro in soup
jokes.punchline: Regis matter-of-factly noting there's no cilantro while chaos erupts
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment with cooking show being invaded by cartoon characters - random ingredient focus

dialogue.speaker: TV Host
dialogue.line: This cilantro in the soup is absolutely delicious!

dialogue.speaker: Regis
dialogue.line: There's no cilantro in this soup.

dialogue.speaker: TV Host
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Regis
dialogue.line: No cilantro.

jokes.text: Aw, somebody's in love. That means you'll have to be neutered.
jokes.setup: Snowball falling for alien cat
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to pet sterilization
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy connecting romance to medical procedure

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, Snowball's falling for an alien cat?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yep, and we gotta get him neutered before he reproduces with it.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That's so messed up, Dad.

jokes.text: Here comes the flying saucer. What's wrong with Stinky? She's teething. Look. Her very first baby tooth.
jokes.setup: Playing airplane game with baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Revealing Maggie has grown an alien-like tooth
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for alien baby reveal - normal parenting moment becomes horror movie discovery

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What is that? Marge, look at Maggie's tooth!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my God! It looks like... like an alien!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We have to call a dentist. Or the government. I'm not sure which one handles this.

jokes.text: Look, Marge! Maggie lost her baby legs.
jokes.setup: Continuing alien baby transformation theme
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual observation about Maggie losing her legs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of alien transformation with Homer's inappropriately casual tone about body horror

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, where'd Maggie's legs go?

jokes.text: Fire, and lots of it. Oh, that's your cure for everything.
jokes.setup: Asked what to prescribe for alien baby
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick suggests fire, gets called out for always suggesting fire
jokes.explanation: Good Dr. Nick character moment - establishing his one-note medical approach of burning everything

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Have you tried fire?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: You always suggest fire!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Nick Riviera
dialogue.line: Well, fire is the cleanser of all things.

jokes.text: Oh, that's your cure for everything.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert suggesting fire
jokes.punchline: Revealing this is his standard response
jokes.explanation: Decent callback revealing character pattern but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Have you tried setting yourself on fire?

dialogue.speaker: Patient
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, it cures a lot of things.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But... wouldn't that make things worse?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, it's my standard response to most problems.

jokes.text: Holy flirking shnit. What's the message?
jokes.setup: Alien receiving transmission from Earth
jokes.punchline: Censored alien swearing with made-up words
jokes.explanation: Decent alien language humor with creative pseudo-profanity that sounds appropriately alien yet familiar

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: Blortch! Krell-dammit! Zyx-shlap! Blorfing Qwerty-holes!

jokes.text: Hello- Oh, great. Mormons. Actually, we're Quantum Presbyterians.
jokes.setup: Homer sees visitors at door and assumes religious solicitors
jokes.punchline: Aliens correct him with absurd denomination name
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with invented religious denomination. Strong structure and quotable phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh no, not the Jehovah's Witnesses again.

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: We are not Witnesses.

dialogue.speaker: Kodos
dialogue.line: We are Jehovah's Refs.

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: We make sure everyone follows the rules.

jokes.text: You intergalactic hussy! How could you?
jokes.setup: Learning Kang is Maggie's father
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic accusation using 'intergalactic hussy'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Homer's over-the-top reaction and creative alien-specific insult

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You intergalactic hussy!

jokes.text: Warning! Warning! Prepare to be abducted.
jokes.setup: Flashback to Marge's alien abduction
jokes.punchline: Polite advance warning defeats purpose of abduction
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - considerate alien following proper protocol for illegal activity.

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: We are here to abduct you for scientific experiments.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh my!

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: We apologize for the inconvenience and any trauma this may cause. We will return you safely within 72 hours, and you will receive complimentary Frequent Abductee miles.

jokes.text: You may choose either the backseat of a Camaro... an airplane bathroom, a friend's wedding... or the alley behind a porno theater.
jokes.setup: Aliens offering reproductive location choices
jokes.punchline: List of stereotypically sleazy human mating locations
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of inappropriate locations with alien clinical presentation of human seediness

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: We have taken the liberty of researching human mating locations. You may choose from the following: a used car lot, a peep show, or the New Jersey Turnpike.

jokes.text: I absolutely refuse to go along with this. But since I have no choice, I'll take the alley.
jokes.setup: Marge's moral stance against alien breeding program
jokes.punchline: Immediately compromises and picks the sleaziest option
jokes.explanation: Great character subversion - Marge's moral objection immediately followed by practical resignation to worst option

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I absolutely refuse to participate in any alien breeding program. It's immoral and wrong!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Although... if I absolutely had to choose... I suppose that one over there is rather handsome.

jokes.text: Really? That seemed awfully quick. What are you implying? Nothing. Nothing.
jokes.setup: Alien breeding procedure completing quickly
jokes.punchline: Marge's implied criticism of alien sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Solid sexual humor with Marge inadvertently insulting alien performance - Kang's defensive response sells it

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Is that it?

dialogue.speaker: Kang
dialogue.line: What do you mean, 'Is that it?' We just got started!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh... I thought you were done.

jokes.text: It made me angry,Jerry. Angry and tired.
jokes.setup: Jerry Springer asking about Homer's feelings about alien affair
jokes.punchline: Homer's understated response to cosmic betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - massive emotional betrayal reduced to simple physical states

dialogue.speaker: Jerry Springer
dialogue.line: Homer, how do you feel about your wife's affair with an alien?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, Jerry, I'm not happy about it. But I guess it could be worse.

jokes.text: Don't forget Ken Starr!
jokes.setup: Aliens destroying political leaders in Washington
jokes.punchline: Bart's helpful reminder to include controversial prosecutor
jokes.explanation: Decent topical humor referencing contemporary political figure - Bart's enthusiastic participation in destruction

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Hey, while you're at it, don't forget to zap that controversial prosecutor!

tmdb_overview: In "Hell Toupée," Snake is sent to the electric chair, and his hair is transplanted onto Homer. In "The Terror of Tiny Toon," Bart and Lisa become cartoon characters in an episode of Itchy & Scratchy. In "Starship Poopers," a growth spurt casts doubt on Maggie's paternity, and the family goes on the Jerry Springer show.
insight: Three-segment horror parody delivers mid-tier laughs at 2.2 jokes per minute.
